name: docker-pkg

metadata.name: docker-pkg

metadata.summary: Build docker images programmatically from templates.

documentation: docker-pkg-images builds docker images from templates.

modules.name: docker_pkg.dockerfile
documentation:
Dockerfile.template processing

The files are Jinja2 templates, the class provides built-in templates to ease
writing Dockerfiles.

methods.name: docker_pkg.drivers.DockerDriver.do_build
documentation:
Builds the image

Parameters:
build_path - context where the build must be performed
filename - the file to output the generated dockerfile to

Returns the image label
Raises an error if the build fails

methods.name: docker_pkg.image.DockerImage._get_author
documentation:
Gets the author name and email for an update.
It uses the DEBFULLNAME and DEBEMAIL env variables if available, else
it reverts to using git configuration data. Finally, a fallback is used,
provided by the docker-pkg configuration.

methods.name: docker_pkg.builder.ImageFSM.all_children
documentation:
Report all images that are born of the current one.

The result will include the current image, and all of their direct and
indirect children. No logical loop protection is present here, nor the
ordering is guaranteed in any ways.

Returns: (list) A list of all images that include the current one.
Finite state machine

classes.name: docker_pkg.builder.DockerBuilder

methods.name: docker_pkg.builder.DockerBuilder._matches_glob
documentation: Check if the label of an image matches the glob pattern

methods.name: docker_pkg.builder.DockerBuilder.scan
documentation:
Scan the desired directory for dockerfiles, add them all to a build chain

max_workers: maximum number of threads to use when scanning local
definition of images. For each image found, `scan` triggers queries
to the local Docker daemon and the registry. Passed to
concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(). Default: 1.
